  "findings": [
    "Single-wall metal vent pipe lacks the required 6-inch clearance to combustible materials."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Shut down the furnace if immediate fire hazard exists, or modify the venting system to maintain a minimum 6-inch clearance to combustibles.",
    "Alternatively, replace the single-wall vent with a Type B double-wall vent to reduce the required clearance to 1 inch."
  ],
  "rationale": "Single-wall metal vent connectors for residential gas furnaces require a minimum clearance of 6 inches to combustible materials under IRC Chapter 18 and NFPA 54. Operating the furnace with insufficient clearance poses a severe fire hazard due to pyrolysis of nearby wood or drywall.",
  "references": [
    "IRC Section M1803.3.4",
    "NFPA 54 Chapter 12"
  ]
}
